title.en: Tomorrow vs Mornings vs This morning

shortcut: morgen = tomorrow (most common) OR morning (rarely, in compounds). 's morgens = in the mornings (recurring habit). vanmorgen / vanochtend = this morning (specific).
explanation: The Dutch word 'morgen' is busier than it looks. It usually means 'tomorrow', but it ALSO means 'morning' — and the distinction depends on what wraps around it. Meanwhile, ''s morgens' is a separate idiom for 'in the mornings' (the recurring kind), and 'vanmorgen' is a third form for 'this morning' (specific). Belgian Dutch class will throw all three at you in the same sentence. Here are the rules for telling them apart.

examples: When 'morgen' stands alone as a time word, it almost always means 'tomorrow'. This is the most common usage.
Common phrases using 'morgen' = tomorrow:
Tot morgen! — See you tomorrow!
Morgen is het zondag. — Tomorrow is Sunday.
Overmorgen — the day after tomorrow.
Eergisteren — the day before yesterday (the parallel for the past).

''s morgens' is the adverb form used for SOMETHING THAT HAPPENS EVERY MORNING — a habit, routine, or general truth. The 's is a fossilised genitive (old form of 'des morgens', meaning 'of the morning').

examples.caption: 'In the mornings I drink coffee.' (every morning, recurring)

examples: Other 'recurring' time adverbs in the same family:
's Ochtends — in the (early) mornings (synonym of 's morgens)
's Middags — in the afternoons
's Avonds — in the evenings
's Nachts — at night
All use the same 's pattern. All describe RECURRING action.
⚠ Apostrophe BEFORE the s — write 's morgens, not s'morgens.

patterns.rule: 3. vanmorgen / vanochtend = THIS MORNING (specific, the one just past or starting)
examples: When you mean 'this morning' specifically — the one just past, or the one we're currently in — use 'vanmorgen' or 'vanochtend' (synonyms). Van + morgen / ochtend.

examples.caption: 'This morning I drank coffee.' (specific morning, just past)

examples: Compare the trio in one row:
Vanmorgen heb ik koffie gedronken. — THIS MORNING I drank coffee. (specific, completed)
's Morgens drink ik koffie. — IN THE MORNINGS I drink coffee. (every morning, habit)
Morgen drink ik koffie. — TOMORROW I'll drink coffee. (future).
↳ 'vanavond' and 'vannacht' work the same way for 'this evening' and 'tonight (late)'.

patterns.rule: 4. de morgen / de ochtend — the noun 'morning'
examples: If you need 'morning' as an actual noun (with an article), use 'de ochtend' (more common) or 'de morgen' (poetic / dated). Both are 'de'-words.
een mooie ochtend — a beautiful morning
de ochtendkrant — the morning paper
de ochtendpost — the morning post
in de ochtend (less common than 's ochtends) — in the morning, as a single occurrence
↳ In everyday speech, learners almost never need 'de morgen' as a noun. Reach for 'de ochtend' if you need it, and for the recurring adverb form use 's ochtends / 's morgens.

patterns.rule: 5. Quick disambiguation guide — which form do I want?
examples: Ask yourself: WHEN does the action happen, and is it a one-off or a recurring pattern?
❌ 'Morgen drink ik koffie' meaning 'in the mornings I drink coffee'. → ✅ ''s Morgens drink ik koffie.' ('morgen' alone = tomorrow, not 'every morning'.)
❌ Writing 's morgens' as 's'morgens' or 'smorgens'. → ✅ ''s morgens' — apostrophe BEFORE the s, space after.
❌ Using 'in de morgen' as a recurring habit. → ✅ Use ''s morgens' for recurring; 'in de ochtend' is fine but less idiomatic.
❌ Confusing 'morgen' (tomorrow) with 'de morgen' (the morning as noun). → Same word, different role — context tells you. The bare 'Morgen…' at the start of a sentence = tomorrow.

memory: 🧠 Three different concepts, three different Dutch forms: TOMORROW = morgen. EVERY MORNING = 's morgens. THIS MORNING (specific) = vanmorgen. Don't confuse the bare 'morgen' (tomorrow) with 's morgens (every morning) — they look related but mean different things.

examples.en: TOMORROW I'm going to Brussels. (future, one-off)
IN THE MORNINGS I always drink coffee. (recurring habit)

examples.nl: Vanmorgen heb ik spek en eieren gegeten.
examples.en: THIS MORNING I ate bacon and eggs. (specific, just past)
It was a beautiful morning. ('ochtend' as a noun)

practice.nl: Wat is het verschil tussen 'morgen' en ''s morgens'?
practice.en: What's the difference between 'morgen' and ''s morgens'?

practice.nl: 'Morgen' alleen = tomorrow. ''s Morgens' = in the mornings (recurrend). 'Morgen drink ik koffie' = ik ga morgen koffie drinken. ''s Morgens drink ik koffie' = elke ochtend.
practice.en: 'Morgen' alone = tomorrow. ''s Morgens' = in the mornings (recurring). 'Morgen drink ik koffie' = I'll drink coffee tomorrow. ''s Morgens drink ik koffie' = every morning.

practice.nl: Hoe zeg je 'this morning I drank coffee'?
practice.en: How do you say 'this morning I drank coffee'?

practice.nl: 'Vanmorgen heb ik koffie gedronken.' Of: 'Vanochtend heb ik koffie gedronken.' Beide zijn correct — 'vanmorgen' en 'vanochtend' zijn synoniemen.
practice.en: 'Vanmorgen heb ik koffie gedronken' or 'Vanochtend heb ik koffie gedronken'. Both correct — 'vanmorgen' and 'vanochtend' are synonyms.

practice.nl: Welke vorm hoort bij 'every evening I watch TV'?
practice.en: Which form goes with 'every evening I watch TV'?

practice.nl: ''s Avonds kijk ik tv.' — recurring, dus de 's-vorm. 'Vanavond kijk ik tv' zou betekenen 'tonight (one specific evening)'.
practice.en: ''s Avonds kijk ik tv.' — recurring, so the 's-form. 'Vanavond kijk ik tv' would mean 'tonight (one specific evening)'.

practice.nl: Waar staat de apostrof bij ''s morgens'?
practice.en: Where does the apostrophe go in ''s morgens'?

practice.nl: VOOR de s. ''s morgens' — niet 's'morgens', niet 'smorgens'. De apostrof staat in voor het oude 'des' (genitief van 'de morgen').
practice.en: BEFORE the s. ''s morgens' — not 's'morgens', not 'smorgens'. The apostrophe stands in for old 'des' (genitive of 'de morgen').
